results.name: Djibouti
results.key_finding: A UN-backed IPC alert projects 256,000 people (25% of the population) at crisis-level hunger, but verified gains in universal health insurance (40%+ coverage) and school enrolment offset it; score holds.

results.name: HCA Healthcare
results.key_finding: North Carolina AG litigation and a Wake Forest study document staffing cuts and care degradation at Mission Health, and HCA dismissed the research rather than investigating it.

results.name: Freeport-McMoRan
results.key_finding: Eight worker deaths in ten months (7 at Grasberg, 1 at Morenci) sit alongside genuine third-party assurance (Copper Mark, ICMM, HRIAs); placeholder looks too low but the trigger is negative, so routed to calibration.

results.name: Zimbabwe
results.key_finding: Constitutional Amendment No. 3 abolished direct presidential election and delayed polls to 2030, but the published score already assumed impunity; no movement.

results.name: State Street
results.key_finding: The -12.7 gap rests on the board-diversity retreat that the documented 2026-04-20 baseline already weighed; treated as assessor variance, not decline.

results.name: Fidelity Investments
results.key_finding: Data-breach settlement remedies were reasonable and community investment is real; the -11.3 gap is the flat-3.5 placeholder versus a researched 3.05 average.

results.name: State Farm
results.key_finding: California regulators FOUND 398 violations of state law in 114 of 220 sampled wildfire claims, and Oklahoma alleges a designed claim-suppression scheme across ~900 suits.

results.name: Thermo Fisher Scientif
results.key_finding: Multi-site closures (421 at Weaverville) with long notice, against strong CSR; scan trigger site (Middleton WI) appears misattributed to Thermo Fisher.

results.name: Kimberly-Clark
results.key_finding: A .25M 401(k) fee settlement at ~15% of losses is a real but minor governance failure; sanitation and period-poverty access programmes push Equity above the placeholder.

results.name: Deere & Company
results.key_finding: The FTC right-to-repair settlement plus a 9M farmer class settlement are genuine positives, offset by US layoffs alongside Mexican expansion; delta within threshold.

results.name: TransDigm
results.key_finding: DoD Inspector General findings of up to 4,451% profit margins and an acquire-then-hike business model confirm the Critical band; placeholder was already positioned correctly.

results.name: Bangladesh
results.key_finding: The Gopalganj trigger describes July 2025 events predating the documented 2026-06-16 upgrade; labour-reform gains hold and no downgrade is warranted.

results.name: Pakistan
results.key_finding: Operation Shaban casualty figures remain security-source-only into a fifth week; independent reconstruction lands within 1.6 points of the 2026-07-18 confirmation.

results.name: Egypt
results.key_finding: HRW documents thousands of refugee arrests and deportations, yet Takaful and Karama reaches 4.66M households; placeholder looks too low but trigger is negative, so routed to calibration.

results.name: Core Civic
results.key_finding: Citizens Bank exiting credit facilities is a third-party judgment, not CoreCivic conduct; 30 of 40 subdimensions score 1 of 5 and the score holds at 7.0.

large_deltas_needing_human_review.note: Two-band drop on tier-5 California regulatory FINDING of 398 legal violations. Published 60.9 is a never-assessed placeholder so part of the gap is scrutiny bias. Reviewer should consider a peer-calibrated landing zone in the Developing band rather than applying 13.8 raw.
Positive gap on a negative trigger (worker fatality). Deliberately NOT proposed as an upgrade per screening rule 3; routed to calibration review.

systemic_finding: Six never-assessed Fortune 500 entities carrying flat ~3.5 placeholder dimensions (Fidelity 62.5, State Farm 60.9, Thermo Fisher 60.9, Kimberly-Clark 60.9, plus State Street 60.2) researched at 47-51 composite, excluding State Farm where genuine regulatory findings apply. This is the documented scrutiny bias, not institutional decline, and is surfaced as a cohort calibration issue rather than as individual downgrades.
scan_data_corrections: Thermo Fisher Scientific: scan cited a Middleton, Wisconsin layoff; that closure appears to belong to Sun Nuclear Corp (Mirion Medical). The underlying restructuring pattern is real (Weaverville NC 421, Franklin MA, Princeton NJ 113).
Bangladesh: scan framed the Gopalganj killings as 2026-07-16; the clashes occurred 2025-07-16 and are described in HRW World Report 2026, which was published in-window.
